tex:
This is Edha's method to work out $99 \times 27$ without using a calculator.
\newline
\[
\begin{aligned}
99 \times 27 &= 100 \times 27 - 27 \\
&= 2700 - 27 \\
&= 2673
\end{aligned}
\]
Show how to use Edha's method to work out $99 \times 68$ without using a calculator.
\newline

stem.content:
This is Edha's method to work out $99 \times 27$ without using a calculator.
\newline
\[
\begin{aligned}
99 \times 27 &= 100 \times 27 - 27 \\
&= 2700 - 27 \\
&= 2673
\end{aligned}
\]
Show how to use Edha's method to work out $99 \times 68$ without using a calculator.
\newline

_rawBody:
This is Edha's method to work out $99 \times 27$ without using a calculator.
\[
\begin{aligned}
99 \times 27 &= 100 \times 27 - 27 \\
&= 2700 - 27 \\
&= 2673
\end{aligned}
\]
Show how to use Edha's method to work out $99 \times 68$ without using a calculator.

\Answerspace[2cm]
\Marks{2}

qtype: finding numbers given HCF and LCM
tex:
Udita thinks of two whole numbers.
\newline
 \\
Both numbers are greater than 6.
\newline
 \\

\newline
 \\
The highest common factor (HCF) of the two numbers is 6.
\newline
 \\
Find the two numbers.
\newline
